req.title: Lab order routing (negative)
req.statement: A clinician shall order a lab test; orders shall route to the patient's preferred lab when available. Negative paths including permission denial, missing mandatory elements, and conflicting state transitions must be tested.
error_pathways: fallback to default lab when preferred unavailable

tests.name: naive_case

doc._raw_response:
```python
"""
Pytest test suite for Lab Test Ordering Requirements
Requirement: A clinician shall order a lab test; orders shall route to the patient's
preferred lab when available.
Covers: happy path, preferred lab routing, permission denial,
missing mandatory elements, and conflicting state transitions.
"""

import pytest
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, call
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
from enum import Enum, auto
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import Optional, List


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Domain models (stand-ins for the real application layer)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class OrderStatus(Enum):
    DRAFT = auto()
    SUBMITTED = auto()
    ROUTED = auto()
    IN_PROGRESS = auto()
    COMPLETED = auto()
    CANCELLED = auto()
    REJECTED = auto()


class UserRole(Enum):
    CLINICIAN = auto()
    NURSE = auto()
    BILLING_CLERK = auto()
    PATIENT = auto()
    ADMIN = auto()


@dataclass
class LabFacility:
    lab_id: str
    name: str
    supported_tests: List[str]
    is_active: bool = True


@dataclass
class Patient:
    patient_id: str
    name: str
    preferred_lab_id: Optional[str] = None
    allergies: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)


@dataclass
class User:
    user_id: str
    role: UserRole
    name: str
    is_active: bool = True


@dataclass
class LabOrder:
    order_id: str
    patient_id: str
    ordering_clinician_id: str
    test_codes: List[str]
    priority: str                       # ROUTINE | STAT | URGENT
    clinical_indication: str
    specimen_type: str
    status: OrderStatus = OrderStatus.DRAFT
    routed_lab_id: Optional[str] = None
    created_at: datetime = field(default_factory=datetime.utcnow)
    submitted_at: Optional[datetime] = None
    notes: Optional[str] = None


class PermissionDeniedError(PermissionError):
    pass

"""
Pytest test suite for Lab Test Ordering Requirements
Requirement: A clinician shall order a lab test; orders shall route to the patient's
preferred lab when available.
Covers: happy path, preferred lab routing, permission denial,
missing mandatory elements, and conflicting state transitions.
"""

import pytest
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, call
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
from enum import Enum, auto
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import Optional, List


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Domain models (stand-ins for the real application layer)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class OrderStatus(Enum):
    DRAFT = auto()
    SUBMITTED = auto()
    ROUTED = auto()
    IN_PROGRESS = auto()
    COMPLETED = auto()
    CANCELLED = auto()
    REJECTED = auto()


class UserRole(Enum):
    CLINICIAN = auto()
    NURSE = auto()
    BILLING_CLERK = auto()
    PATIENT = auto()
    ADMIN = auto()


@dataclass
class LabFacility:
    lab_id: str
    name: str
    supported_tests: List[str]
    is_active: bool = True


@dataclass
class Patient:
    patient_id: str
    name: str
    preferred_lab_id: Optional[str] = None
    allergies: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)


@dataclass
class User:
    user_id: str
    role: UserRole
    name: str
    is_active: bool = True


@dataclass
class LabOrder:
    order_id: str
    patient_id: str
    ordering_clinician_id: str
    test_codes: List[str]
    priority: str                       # ROUTINE | STAT | URGENT
    clinical_indication: str
    specimen_type: str
    status: OrderStatus = OrderStatus.DRAFT
    routed_lab_id: Optional[str] = None
    created_at: datetime = field(default_factory=datetime.utcnow)
    submitted_at: Optional[datetime] = None
    notes: Optional[str] = None


class PermissionDeniedError(PermissionError):
    pass


class MissingMandatoryFieldError(ValueError):
    pass


class InvalidStateTransitionError(RuntimeError):
    pass


class PreferredLabUnavailableError(RuntimeError):
    pass


class LabNotFoundError(LookupError):
    pass


class DuplicateOrderError(RuntimeError):
    pass


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Service under test (interface / contract)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class LabOrderService:
    """
    Application service that encapsulates ordering logic.
    All dependencies are injected so they can be replaced with mocks.
    """

    MANDATORY_FIELDS = ["patient_id", "ordering_clinician_id", "test_codes",
                        "priority", "clinical_indication", "specimen_type"]
    VALID_PRIORITIES = {"ROUTINE", "STAT", "URGENT"}
    ALLOWED_ROLES = {UserRole.CLINICIAN}

    def __init__(self, order_repo, patient_repo, lab_repo,
                 auth_service, notification_service, audit_service):
        self._order_repo = order_repo
        self._patient_repo = patient_repo
        self._lab_repo = lab_repo
        self._auth = auth_service
        self._notifier = notification_service
        self._audit = audit_service

    # -- public API ----------------------------------------------------------

    def create_order(self, requesting_user: User, order_data: dict) -> LabOrder:
        self._check_permission(requesting_user, "create_order")
        self._validate_mandatory_fields(order_data)
        self._validate_priority(order_data["priority"])

        patient = self._patient_repo.get_by_id(order_data["patient_id"])
        order = LabOrder(
            order_id=self._order_repo.next_id(),
            patient_id=patient.patient_id,
            ordering_clinician_id=requesting_user.user_id,
            test_codes=order_data["test_codes"],
            priority=order_data["priority"],
            clinical_indication=order_data["clinical_indication"],
            specimen_type=order_data["spec
